generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands into motor voltage commands via the Vedder Electronic Speed Controller to control the vehicle's speed."
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls the steering angle of the vehicle, interfaced through ROS2 nodes and implemented in C++ for lifecycle management."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Captures 3D point clouds of the vehicle's surroundings using Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR, processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides depth perception and additional environmental data using Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraDepthData
]

instance EgoVehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuously monitors the ego vehicle's speed in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EgoVehicleSpeedData
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuously monitors the ego vehicle's steering angle in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
]

instance CameraDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
]

instance EgoVehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EgoVehicleSpeedSensor
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DBSCANClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Combines LiDAR point clouds and camera data for object tracking using DBSCAN clustering algorithm."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Tracks objects using Kalman filters for state estimation based on sensor fusion outputs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance ACCControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements ISO 15622 performance class I ACC logic (speed adjustment based on lead vehicle distance)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCControlEnabler
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stores time-series data including ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle) and object tracks in an SQL database for reporting and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EgoVehicleSpeedData, SteeringAngleData, ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageEnabler
]

instance RealTimeEgoVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time ego vehicle state data including speed, steering angle, and sensor fusion outputs for ACC functionality."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EgoVehicleSpeedData, SteeringAngleData, ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks from raw LiDAR point clouds clustered via DBSCAN and tracked via Kalman filters."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData, CameraDepthData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes raw LiDAR point clouds and camera data using DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance ACCControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements ACC logic based on ISO 15622 performance class I, handling speed adjustment and lead vehicle detection."
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCComputationService
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables real-time visualization of ego vehicle state, object tracks, and projected driving path via 3D visualization interface."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Manages storage of time-series data in SQL database for reporting and analysis, including ego vehicle state and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataAnalysisService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables remote enabling/disabling of ACC, setting desired speed, and issuing emergency braking commands via Control Panel interface."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integrates application modules and enables inter-node communication for the Digital Twin system."
]

instance WiFiCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates inter-entity communication between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity with ≤100 ms end-to-end latency."
]

instance NVIDIAJetpackEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides hardware SDK for programming the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer."
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle), object tracks, and projected driving path via 3D Visualization interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimePerformanceInsight, DrivingPathVisualizationInsight
]

instance ACCComputationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC system computation (lead vehicle detection and speed computation) from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCPerformanceInsight, ComputationOffloadingAction
]

instance HistoricalDataAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Analyzes time-series data stored in SQL database for trend analysis and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalTrendInsight, PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables remote enabling/disabling of ACC, setting desired speed, and issuing emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction, ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Tracks objects in real-time using sensor fusion from LiDAR and camera data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectDetectionInsight, ObjectTrackingInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimePerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time performance metrics including ego vehicle speed, steering angle, and object tracks for assessing ACC functionality."
]

instance DrivingPathVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D CAD-like representation of ego vehicle state, object tracks, and driving paths for visualization."
]

instance ACCPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance metrics of the ACC system based on lead vehicle distance and speed adjustment."
]

instance HistoricalTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Trend analysis of time-series data stored in SQL database for predictive maintenance."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance insights derived from historical data analysis."
]

instance ObjectDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of objects in the vehicle's surroundings using sensor fusion."
]

instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Tracking of detected objects using Kalman filters for state estimation."
]

instance ComputationOffloadingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC computation from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity to reduce computational burden."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables remote enabling/disabling of ACC and setting desired speed."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts ACC parameters (e.g., desired speed, braking thresholds) remotely via Control Panel."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world include:
    - **Desired speed commands**: Computed by the **ACC Controlling FE** in the **Digital Twin Entity**, transmitted back to the **Actuation FE** in the **Physical Twin Entity**, and executed via the **Vedder Electronic Speed Controller** to adjust the vehicle's speed.
    - **Remote management commands**: Sent from the **User Entity** (e.g., enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speed, or emergency braking) via the **Control Panel** and **User Interface FE**, processed by the **Digital Twin Entity**, and relayed to the **Actuation FE** for execution.
    - **Actuation signals**: Generated in response to user commands or ACC logic, transmitted over **Wi-Fi** and executed on the vehicle's hardware."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates on a **real-time time-scale** with the following constraints:
    - **End-to-end latency**: ≤ **100 ms** (as per 3GPP TS 22.185 for V2V communication).
    - **Clock synchronization**: Devices must be synchronized to an accuracy of **±100 ms** to ensure event ordering and data integrity in distributed operations.
    The **Data Collecting FE** and **Data Translation FE** ensure timely data transmission between the **Physical Twin Entity** and **Digital Twin Entity**, while the **ACC Controlling FE** processes data in real-time to compute and send desired speed commands."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation is orchestrated as follows:
    - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensors (LiDAR, camera), actuators (motor, steering servo), and pre-processing modules (**Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**).
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosts core DT logic (**ACC Controlling FE**, **Data Collecting FE**, **Reporting FE**, **Presentation FE**) and is deployed on external hardware (e.g., cloud or edge server).
    - **User Entity**: Provides **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** interfaces for monitoring and remote management.
    - **Cross-System Entity**: **Data Translation FE** ensures seamless data exchange between entities.
    The constellation adheres to the **ISO 23247 RA** with modifications, including the addition of the **Physical Twin Entity** and decomposition of the **User Interface FE** into subcomponents."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is primarily used during the **operation phase** of the vehicle's lifecycle, supporting:
    - **Real-time monitoring and control** of the ACC system.
    - **Cyber-physical experimentation** for validation and optimization of ADAS functionalities.
    - **Predictive maintenance and health monitoring** (e.g., brake system prognosis) via integrated simulators like **CREO Simulate**.
    The DT also supports **product lifecycle management** by maintaining a **digital thread** of vehicle data from design to operation, though the case study focuses on **as-operated** representations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
    - **Real-time data synchronization**: Achieved via **ROS2** and **Wi-Fi** communication with **≤100 ms latency**.
    - **Model validation**: ACC logic adheres to **ISO 15622** standards for performance class I systems.
    - **Sensor fusion accuracy**: **DBSCAN clustering** and **Kalman filtering** improve object track fidelity.
    - **Verification mechanisms**: Data is cross-validated between **Physical Twin Entity** and **Digital Twin Entity** via the **Data Translation FE**.
    - **Uncertainty management**: Clock synchronization (±100 ms) and latency constraints mitigate timing-related errors in distributed operations."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the **Physical Twin (PT)** and **Digital Twin (DT)** involves:
    - **Network protocol**: **Wi-Fi** for inter-entity communication (PT → DT and DT → PT).
    - **Intra-entity communication**: **ROS2 topics** for message passing between software components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE** to **Data Collecting FE**).
    - **Data translation**: Custom **Data Translation FE** handles serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion.
    - **Latency requirements**: End-to-end communication must adhere to **≤100 ms** (as per 3GPP TS 22.185 for V2V communication)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is hosted and deployed as follows:
    - **Physical Twin Entity**: Deployed on the **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** (onboard the vehicle) for real-time sensor data processing and actuation.
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Deployed on **external hardware** (e.g., cloud or edge server) to offload computation-intensive tasks like ACC logic and data storage.
    - **User Entity**: Hosted on a **remote workstation** for visualization and remote management via **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** interfaces.
    The deployment follows a **modular ROS2-based architecture**, where each entity is implemented as a set of **ROS2 packages**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration involves:
    - **Data exchange with external systems**: Not explicitly detailed in the document, but implied for **product lifecycle management** (e.g., integration with **PLM tools** for digital threads).
    - **Interoperability**: The use of **ROS2** and **ISO 23247 RA** facilitates integration with other DT systems or automotive tools (e.g., **MATLAB/Simulink** for simulation).
    - **Standardized interfaces**: **Data Translation FE** ensures compatibility with external data formats and protocols."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin adheres to the following standards:
    - **ISO 23247 (Part 2)**: Reference architecture for Digital Twin development, adapted for automotive use (e.g., mapping software components to **Functional Entities** like **Data Collecting FE**, **Controlling FE**).
    - **ISO 15622**: Standard for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** performance class I, guiding the implementation of ACC logic.
    - **3GPP TS 22.185**: Specifies **≤100 ms end-to-end latency** for V2V communication, informing the DT's real-time requirements.
    - **ROS2**: Middleware standard for integrating autonomous systems and enabling modular communication."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
    - **Real-time constraints**: The system enforces **≤100 ms latency** and **±100 ms clock synchronization** to ensure timely and coordinated operations, critical for safety-critical ADAS functionalities.
    - **Remote operation risks**: The **Control Panel** allows remote management (e.g., emergency braking), which must be validated to prevent unintended accidents. The system is classified as a **Fully Autonomous Digital Twin with Human-in-the-Loop** (per David and Bork’s taxonomy).
    - **Data integrity**: The **Data Translation FE** ensures accurate data exchange between entities, reducing risks of corrupted or delayed commands.
    - **Hardware limitations**: Clock synchronization accuracy is constrained to **±100 ms** due to hardware limitations, which may impact safety in stricter applications. Future work could explore stricter synchronization standards."
]
